quoteFirst: In 1472 or 1473 a son was added to the family, and the parents had a horoscope taken of the child, who appeared at
quoteLast: on January 19, 1472, according to some; at forty-eight minutes past four in the afternoon of February 19, 1473, according to others; the exact instant of the nativity being an important point in astrological calculations, which, in those days, inspired in fathers and mothers the deepest concern.
title:
Great Men and Famous Women. Vol. 3
A series of pen and pencil sketches of the lives of more than 200 of the most prominent personages in History
author: Horne, Charles F. (Charles Francis), 1870-1942 [Editor]

quoteLast: _Southampton_ reported the enemy's Battle Fleet ahead. The destroyers were recalled, and at 4.42 p.m. the enemy's Battle Fleet was sighted SE. Course was altered 16 points in succession to starboard, and I proceeded on a northerly course to lead them towards the Battle Fleet. The enemy battle-cruisers altered course shortly afterwards, and the action continued. _Southampton_, with the 2nd Light-cruiser Squadron, held on to the southward to observe. They closed to within 13,000 yards of the enemy Battle Fleet, and came under a very heavy but ineffective fire. _Southampton's_ reports were most valuable. The 5th Battle
title: World's War Events, Vol. II
author: Reynolds, Francis J. (Francis Joseph), 1867-1937 [Editor]; Churchill, Allen L. (Allen Leon), 1873-1940 [Editor]

quoteFirst: Whether Fudge’s watch was slow or whether, absorbed in their conversation, they consumed more time than they realized on the way, the City Hall clock proclaimed
quoteLast: when they reached the Common and, to Fudge’s intense disgust, the theater was out.
title: The Purple Pennant
author: Barbour, Ralph Henry, 1870-1944; Rockwell, Norman, 1894-1978 [Illustrator]
